summary.intent: Implement a DOM-free Tour agnostic core for the ars-ui project, open a draft PR, and address Codex automated review feedback by fixing dynamic step effect emissions and progress text edge cases.
summary.outcome: PR #673 was opened, Codex review completed with 6 actionable threads, all issues were fixed with TDD (red tests first, then implementation), and a clean review-fix commit was pushed to the branch.

repo: The project uses a Codex automated review bot triggered via '@codex review' comment; progress is tracked via GitHub reactions (👀 = in progress, 👍 = approved) and review threads.
The tour state machine uses an effect-based contract where dynamic mutations (RemoveStep, UpdateStep, SyncProps) must emit step-change and geometry effects when the active step changes.
Cargo tasks use custom xtask aliases: 'cargo xci-fast', 'cargo xtask spec validate', and llvm-cov for coverage.
The project uses nightly Rust for formatting ('cargo +nightly fmt --all') separate from regular cargo commands.
Spec conformance tests live in a separate test crate: 'cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance'.
Proptests for state machine invariants are marked #[ignored] and must be run explicitly with '-- --ignored'.

code.finding: Dynamic step mutation transitions (remove/update/sync) were missing effect emissions for step-change and geometry callbacks when the active step changed.
Progress text logic had an edge case: empty tours (zero steps) were rendering '1 of 0' instead of '0 of 0' due to missing bounds check.
SyncProps with empty steps needed to reuse the normal close lifecycle plan rather than silently suppressing effects.

workflow: The receiving-code-review and test-driven-development skills are loaded from ~/.claude/skills/ to guide the fix workflow.
The Codex review loop skill is defined at .agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md with a polling cadence using GitHub reactions as state signals.
TDD approach was enforced: regression tests were added and verified red before patching the implementation.
The PR body must include 'Closes #<issue>' and a 'snapshot-reviewed' label must be added via 'gh pr edit'.
Unrelated local diffs (Dioxus formatter changes) were intentionally kept unstaged to avoid scope creep in the PR.

friction: Commit hook ran silently for an extended period, requiring passive waiting before the push could proceed.
The Codex review took multiple polling cycles (~10+ minutes) before producing threads, requiring repeated sleep/poll loops.
The ignored Tour proptest took long enough to run that fmt and re-verification had to be scheduled around it, causing multiple wait cycles.
The review-loop polling approach using 'sleep 120' is brittle and verbose, producing many near-identical intermediate assistant messages.
open_items: Unrelated Dioxus formatter changes remain as local unstaged diff — needs a separate PR or cleanup.
PR #673 is still in draft state; it needs to be marked ready for review and merged after CI fully passes.
CI jobs (release, coverage, feature-flag groups, adapter parity) were still running at the time of the fix commit — final green CI state was not confirmed in the transcript.
The 'cargo mutants' mutation testing command listed in the PR verification section was not run as part of the review-fix verification pass.
